issue_title: Set New Password Multiple Times via Password Reset Function
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

*Not sure if the area selected is correct*

While it was not possible to use the *"Link to reset credentials"* after the password had been reset successfully, it was possible to change the password a second time if the link was opened in two browsers before the password reset was finished. (This was found in an older version 24.x but also tested in 26.1.1.)

Example link:
`http://localhost:8081/realms/master/login-actions/action-token?key=eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiIsInR5cCIgOiAiSldUIiwia2lkIiA6ICI4NDMxNjMyZS1lOTc0LTQ3ZWYtYWVhZS01NjM4NDJmZTJhNWIifQ..pD4hZLwuvXfPU5Fr7Q90-0yJySBkUDnPULAq3gg2paBSIf5wO8ctt5mNKyMDnX7iGPDHL8gD1i6CHJJrf2uYig&execution=50decf3d-22af-4f31-8bc3-df061016277a&client_id=security-admin-console&tab_id=Yv3KPNiczgY&client_data=eyJydSI6Imh0dHA6Ly9sb2NhbGhvc3Q6ODA4MS9hZG1pbi9tYXN0ZXIvY29uc29sZS8jL3Rlc3QvY2xpZW50cy9hY2ExYTQ3NC0yZjQzLTQ1ZWUtYjU4NS03Yzg1NzhhZTZkZWYvY3JlZGVudGlhbHMiLCJydCI6ImNvZGUiLCJybSI6InF1ZXJ5Iiwic3QiOiJjMjNmODc3Yi0xYmY1LTQ5ODYtODg3OS04ZTczZDI0YzVlMjYifQ`

### Version

26.1.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

It should not be possible to change the password more than once with the same reset link.

### Actual behavior

It appears that the JWT in the password reset link is invalidated after the password is updated. However, if the link is accessed twice before the password reset is finished, both sessions remain active and it is possible to change the password in both sessions. (As long as the JWT in the link is not expired.)

### How to Reproduce?

 1. Create a user and set an email address. 
 2. Enable the "Forgot password" function.
 3. Start a login and select forgot password. 
 4. Enter the username. 
 5. Open the reset link in two different browser sessions.
    1. Change the password in one session -> login with the new password 
      is successful. 
    2. Afterwards change the password in the second session -> login with 
      even newer password is successful.

### Anything else?

**Countermeasure**

We think that the JWT should be send with the final request in the process. Keycloak should validate the JWT when the password change is actually performed and afterwards it should get invalidated.

Reset a password only once

Closes #37231

issue_title: Ensure all resources in the permission is of the same type
issue_body: For permissions containing more than one resource we should ensure all resources are of the same type.
commit_message:
fix: validate resource type consistency when adding resources to FGAP permissions

Previously, getOrCreateResource() would return any resource found by its
authz DB ID without verifying it belonged to the requested resource type.
This allowed resources of one type (e.g. Users) to be silently added to a
permission of a different type (e.g. Groups) by passing the internal authz
resource ID.

- For per-entity resources found by ID, validate the name resolves as the
  expected entity type via getResourceName(); throw ModelValidationException
  on mismatch

Closes #37243

Signed-off-by: Vinit Kumar <30852363+ThreeMangoTrees@users.noreply.github.com>

issue_title: External IDP error during Step-Up Authentication does no longer route back to browser flow
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

Up until Keycloak 26.1 if an external identity provider returned an error during step up authentication (i.e. user is already logged in but needs to increase the LoA via the external idp), Keycloak routed back to the browser flow where we could handle the error, show something to the user etc.
Now Keycloak redirects to the caller (redirect_uri) and provides an error. 

### Version

26.1.2

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

External IDP errors can be handled in the flow rather then returning directly to the client

### Actual behavior

Keycloak redirects to the client without us being able to handle the error

### How to Reproduce?

The easiest way to reproduce this should be a custom browser flow without Cookie Authenticator (to simulate step up authentication even if user is already logged in, without setting up actual step up authentication / custom authenticator handling higher requested LoA) and a single external IDP configured in the realm.
Then log in normally and trigger the login again after you are already logged in. Select the external IDP to authenticate and trigger an error there.

### Anything else?

This bug has been introduced by https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/35728 which removed the client-id check from `checkAccountManagementFailedLinking`

issue_title: Cannot fetch realm role that was renamed
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

When updating the name of a realm role using admin REST API, the server responds with 404 when requested to fetch recently renamed role.

### Version

26.1.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The newly created role should be renamed and fetched when requested.

### Actual behavior

While the role is created and renamed as expected, it cannot be fetched after being renamed. Surprisingly, if you skip step 2 (as described in `How to Reproduce?` section), everything works as expected.

### How to Reproduce?

Steps to reproduce:
0) Get token
```
curl --location '{keycloakUrl}/realms/{realm}/protocol/openid-connect/token' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
--data-urlencode 'client_id={clientId}' \
--data-urlencode 'client_secret={clientSecret}' \
--data-urlencode 'grant_type=client_credentials'
```
1) Create role realm
```
curl --location '{keycloakUrl}/admin/realms/{realm}/roles' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: ••••••' \
--data '{
    "name": {originalName}
}'
```
2) Fetch role with the new name - check for unique name (before updating role)
```
curl --location '{keycloakUrl}/admin/realms/{realm}/roles/{newName}' \
--header 'Authorization: ••••••'
```
3) Update/rename role
```
curl --location --request PUT '{keycloakUrl}/admin/realms/{realm}/roles/{originalName}' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: ••••••' \
--data '{
    "name": {newName}
}'
```
4) Fetch role with new name
```
curl --location '{keycloakUrl}/admin/realms/{realm}/roles/{newName}' \
--header 'Authorization: ••••••'
```

### Anything else?

While everything else works as expected in keycloak UI, when you click the tab `Users in role` that is available on relevant realm role details page, the application crashes and the following attached error message is displayed

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/74eb44f4-850d-4403-b86b-bcc7533ce890)

Fixing incorrectly cached role after update/rename

issue_title: Make sure resources are properly managed when updating permissions
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/fine-grained-permissions

### Describe the bug

When updating permissions the resources are not properly managed leading to unexpected behavior and enforcing access.

The backend should make sure that permissions with more than one resource associated should not contain the resource type resource (e.g.: `Users`) but only resource objects of the same type. Otherwise, the permission will be a mix of `All` and `Specific` permission.

The backend should also make sure that permissions without a resource are always associated with the resource type resource (e.g.: `Users`) so that they apply to all resource objects of the same type.

### Version

999.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Properly update permissions

### Actual behavior

Permissions are inconsistent when updating

### How to Reproduce?

* Create a  `Users` resource type permission that applies to all resources
* Update the permission to enforce access to a specific user/resource

### Anything else?

_No response_

Make sure resources are properly managed when updating permissions

Closes #37337

issue_title: Web Crypto API not available in `login-status-iframe.html` when using admin console from insecure context
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

We expose the admin console only in an internal network over HTTP.
Since it's HTTP, it's considered an "insecure context", and thus the Web Crypto API is not available.
Keycloak, however, allows things to work from insecure context by detecting requests coming from such contexts and including a `web-crypto-shim.js` script to cover that functionality.

However, even though the admin console itself is being accessed from an insecure context and therefore gets the shim script, the silent login that's triggered from the `login-status-iframe.html` hits our public (and of course HTTPS enabled) OIDC endpoint. This is a secure context so the shim is not included in the html response.

Due to that, the admin UI console fails to load when such conditions are met:
 - Keycloak v26.1.2
 - Admin console accessed via http
 - `login-status-iframe.html` accessed via https

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b2c52de6-dce5-4338-ae1c-aae9bcdfb73a)

My guess is that this bug was introduced in here https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/35678/files#diff-9acdbf5dfc241edfb3a31eab1e1223fe2e3bdf80a605da2c5bf2f02cd007cd66 , when the `uuid` generation functionality in the admin console was changed from a custom package to crypto API.

### Version

26.1.2

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I should be able to access the admin console using HTTP.

### Actual behavior

The admin console fails to load when it tries to login using `login-status-iframe.html` with the following error

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/89d1a0f1-2b0d-48a2-84f0-e7239c14784e)

### How to Reproduce?

Expose the admin console in HTTP
Have the OIDC endpoints exposed in HTTPS
Login to the admin console using Firefox.

(I am not entirely sure what triggers the `login-status-iframe.html` to load. I have only seen it in Firefox so far.) 

 

### Anything else?

This has been previously discussed in here https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/36804#issuecomment-2659077295
commit_message:
Detect insecure context for HTTPS iframes embedded in HTTP pages

Closes #37355

Signed-off-by: Jon Koops <jonkoops@gmail.com>

issue_title: CVE-2024-47072 - XStream is vulnerable to a Denial of Service attack due to stack overflow from a manipulated binary input stream
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

_No response_

### Describe the bug

XStream is a simple library to serialize objects to XML and back again. This vulnerability may allow a remote attacker to terminate the application with a stack overflow error resulting in a denial of service only by manipulating the processed input stream when XStream is configured to use the BinaryStreamDriver. XStream 1.4.21 has been patched to detect the manipulation in the binary input stream causing the the stack overflow and raises an InputManipulationException instead. Users are advised to upgrade. Users unable to upgrade may catch the StackOverflowError in the client code calling XStream if XStream is configured to use the BinaryStreamDriver.

https://github.com/x-stream/xstream/security/advisories/GHSA-hfq9-hggm-c56q

### Version

24.0.10

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

No CVEs reported for 24.

### Actual behavior

CVEs reported for 24.

### How to Reproduce?

Switch to 24 branch and check the XStream version.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Organizations: Adding LDAP federated user to org leads to org group being pushed to LDAP
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

When adding a LDAP user to an organization, if the LDAP provider contains a group mapper that is writable (i.e. LDAP_ONLY mode), the organization group ends up being created on LDAP, leading to a series of issues because the group is an internal group that is not meant to be exported to LDAP.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Joining an organization should not result in the organization group being pushed to LDAP.

### Actual behavior

When a LDAP user joins an organization, the organization internal group is pushed to LDAP with a group mapper with LDAP_ONLY mode is used.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create an organization\
2. Create an LDAP federation (Writable, Import Users = ON)
3. Add a group mapper (mode=LDAP_ONLY)
4. Sync the users from LDAP
5. Select the organization, click on Members, then click on invite Realm User
6. Select one of the LDAP imported users -> See that an new group matching the org id was created in LDAP

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Password policies like NoUsername consider case-sensitivity
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

### Original report

Assume there is `Not Username` password policy set.

Concluding my analysis after RHSSO 7.6.9, RHBK 24.0.x, in RHBK 26.0.7 I can obtain the registration of the same strings as 'username' (username = Testing@12345678 / password: Testing@12345678), if 'contain' 'username', and with other strings they are not validated as an invalid 'password'(Success! The password has been set.), that is, it does not respect the password policy.

### Cause

We are doing lowercasing of username before saving them to the DB. So username is lowercase to "testing@12345678" before it is saved. Then the password policy checks if password "Testing@12345678" equals to username "testing@12345678" and it allows password creation due the fact that username was lowercased.

It seems to me this should be fixed for multiple password policies:

- NotUsernamePasswordPolicy
- NotEmailPasswordPolicy
- NotContainsUsernamePasswordPolicy

to make sure that we compare in case-insensitive way.



### Version

nightly (from 2025-02-18), older versions as well from RHSSO 7.6.9

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Password is compared with the username/email in case-insensitive way, so it is not possible to create for example email `Testing123@email.cz` and password `testing123@email.cz` (as it is in fact same email).

### Actual behavior

Case-sensitivity is considered, so it is possible to create for example email `Testing123@email.cz` and password `testing123@email.cz` (even if it is in fact same email).

### How to Reproduce?

See description

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Password policies like NoUsername should compare in case-insensitive way
closes #37431

Signed-off-by: mposolda <mposolda@gmail.com>

issue_title: account-console no longer provides nonce/state parameter
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

With the update from Keycloak 26.0.8 to 26.1.0 I noticed that account-console gave a "Missing parameter: state" error. After some investigation I noticed that this is because we are using the secure-session client policy.

The reason for this seems to be that there has been a change to account-console where it no longer uses the state/nonce parameters and since the secure-session client policy requires these to be there it fails.

I can't find any information about this in the release notes and it feels like a breaking change but I guess it can be discussed. So far I see this as a bug but if not I would love to hear if there is a reason for this. It seems the security-console still uses state/nonce as it should.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/893b5d76-afc8-4dd5-9f94-8e0c97c08521)

### Version

26.1.0

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I would expect that account-console works with secure-session client policy. This means that I would expect it to use state/nonce parameters in the OIDC flow.

Expected URL (Also how it looks in 26.0.8):
http://localhost:8080/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/auth?client_id=account-console&redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A8080%2Frealms%2Ftest%2Faccount%2F&state=9263e110-9720-4ae5-adb7-3d56a7606524&response_mode=query&response_type=code&scope=openid&nonce=cf26e8e4-d51c-4748-9b32-72e556399fd1&code_challenge=fe-QrTtqOlyhbz9So1zSHKW6BpdaGjggE0GscpR5yQ4&code_challenge_method=S256

### Actual behavior

The current behavior is that account-console does not supply state/nonce parameters anymore in the OIDC flow.

Actual URL (How it looks from 26.1.0 and forward):
http://localhost:8080/realms/test/protocol/openid-connect/auth?client_id=account-console&redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A8080%2Frealms%2Ftest%2Faccount&response_type=code&code_challenge=5i6Psu8hD-AwOMkw7wSYnZOHeH5eSDIn_G8qImqOo0o&code_challenge_method=S256

### How to Reproduce?

Spin up a version of Keycloak later than 26.1.0 and enable secure-session client policy.
Realm Settings->Client Policies->Profile

Create a new Profile and add the secure-session executer.

Go back to Realm Settings->Client Policies->Policies

Create a new Policy and add client-any condition. Then add the client profile created and click save.

Logout from admin console, or use incognito mode and go to http://localhost:8080/realms/master/account/

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: High number of false positives in OpenSSF Scorecard
issue_body:
### Description

A high number of false positives are being reported in OpenSSF Scorecard, primarily from test dependencies. After triaging these reports, I identified that most of the flagged vulnerabilities are not relevant. However, there is currently no built-in way to recursively scan paths while using a single ignore file.  

To address this, we need to add ignore files close to the affected paths to ensure that test dependencies do not trigger false positives. This effort aligns with our broader goal of improving our **clomonitor.io** score.  

### References

- https://clomonitor.io/projects/cncf/keycloak
- https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/36127
- https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/32315
- https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/31451
- https://github.com/ossf/scorecard/issues/4530
- https://github.com/google/osv-scanner/issues/1640

issue_title: LDAP group mapper skips configured filter and imports all groups with memberOf strategy when fetching the user's groups
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

When a group mapper is configured with a filter and the `GET_GROUPS_FROM_USER_MEMBEROF_ATTRIBUTE`  strategy, the filter is respected when syncing the LDAP groups into Keycloak. However, if we select an LDAP user in the `Users` screen, and go to `Groups`, the `getGroupsStream()` call ends up fetching all groups it finds under the `memberOf` attribute in the user LDAP entry and imports them, ignoring the configured filter.

### Version

26.1.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Only the groups that match the ldap group mapper filter should be synced

### Actual behavior

All groups found under the `memberOf` attribute in the user entry are synced

### How to Reproduce?

- Start the embedded server (`mvn -f testsuite/utils/pom.xml exec:java -PLdap`)
- Using a tool like Apache Directory Studio, edit the users to reference the groups they belong to using an attribute (instead of the `member` attribute in the groups). For simplicity, add group CNs using a regular attribute like `postalCode` to avoid having to configure the member-of overlay as that attribute is not availble out of the box).
- Create an LDAP provider with a group mapper using the `GET_GROUPS_FROM_USER_MEMBEROF_ATTRIBUTE` strategy  and `Preserve Group Inheritance` set to `Off`. Use the `postalCode` as the memberOf attribute in the config.
- Add a group filter - something like `(cn=ldap-admin)`
- Sync the groups and see that only the `ldap-admin` group was imported
- Go to Users then select a user that belongs to more groups, and then click on Groups
- Check that all groups the user is a member of have been imported, ignoring the filter

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
LDAP group mapper skips configured filter and imports all groups with memberOf strategy when fetching the user's groups

Closes #37537

issue_title: The token exchange grant type not available in well-known endpoint when token-exchange-standard feature enabled
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

token-exchange

### Describe the bug

The OIDC well-known endpoint correctly returns grant type `urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange` when token-exchange feature is enabled (which is token-exchange:v1). That is correct. But this grant type is not returned when token-exchange-standard feature is enabled (new token exchange), which is incorrect.

Figured that `urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:uma-ticket` also not included in the well-known endpoint, but should be.

The cause is, that list of supported grant-types is hardcoded. Will be better when it is dynamically retrieved from available implementations of `OAuth2GrantType` provider. That will make it possible to add custom grant type implementations deployed by customers.


### Version

nightly (from 2025-02-20)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Grant type `urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange`  available in claim `grant_types_supported` if token-exchange-standard feature enabled

### Actual behavior

Grant type `urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange` not available in claim `grant_types_supported` if token-exchange-standard feature enabled

### How to Reproduce?

See above

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Linking user in different browser doesn't work if original window/tab is closed
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area identity-brokering ### Describe the bug When a user links a 3rd party IdP account to an existing account, they are prompted to click on a link in an email to verify their email address. After clicking the link, the user is eventually asked to return to the original browser window/tab and click ‘Continue’ (next to ‘Verified in another browser?’) If they already closed the tab/window they were in, they’re unable to link their account unless they restart the process from scratch. ### Version 26.1.2 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior IDP link should be added after the user is confirmed to proceed by email. When the user tries to log in with iDP that was just linked to account, it should proceed with iDP login, instead of linking the account again. ### Actual behavior The user does not have added an iDP link in the Admin console. The user is prompted about the existing account and has to restart the linking process from scratch. ### How to Reproduce? 1. Prepare an IdP account which already has an existing account. 2. Navigate to the Keycloak login page. 3. Click on the IdP provider link of your choice and login with your IdP account. 5. When prompted about the existing account, select ‘Add to existing account'. ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8416d0b2-af18-4374-bbbc-f6d9d9420eb3) 6.. When prompted to verify your email, close the tab you’re in and open your email in a different browser. 7. Click on the link in the email. 8. Confirm you want to link your IdP account. 9. Reopen your previous browser, navigate to the Keycloak login page, and try to login with your IdP account. ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/98618278-2aaf-4a48-be94-94a63426ae4e) ### Anything else? _No response_

issue_title: Property Name Casing Mismatch in ProtocolMapperUtils
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

We've encountered an issue in [ProtocolMapperUtils](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/ProtocolMapperUtils.java) where property names are incorrectly stored in the ACCESSORS map with a lowercase first letter, causing mismatches when looking up methods.

This appears to be a regression from Keycloak 26.0.0 where this was working correctly. When defining a ProtcolMapper Property with an Uppercase property, like `Email` or `LastName` or `FirstName`, it no longer maps that users property correctly into a saml attribute. The result was a broken IDP connection between keycloak and our saml application.

### Version

26.1.0+

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

- Property names should be stored exactly as extracted without modifying their case.
- `Email` should be stored as `Email` in ACCESSORS, ensuring a correct lookup.
- A protocol mapper configured to reference `Email` should continue to work.

### Actual behavior

- The static block extracts method names from UserModel, correctly identifying properties like `Email` from `getEmail()`.
- However, before storing these in `ACCESSORS`, the first letter is forced to lowercase
```java
propertyName = Character.toLowerCase(propertyName.charAt(0)) + propertyName.substring(1);
ACCESSORS.put(propertyName, method);
```
- Later, when calling `getUserModelValue(user, "Email")`, it attempts:
```java
Method m = ACCESSORS.get("Email");
```
but `Email` does not match `email`, causing the lookup to fail.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Define a protocol mapper that references a user model property with an uppercase letter (e.g., `Email`).
2. Deploy and attempt to retrieve the mapped attribute.
3. Observe that the value is `null` or missing in the token.
4. Modify the protocol mapper configuration to use "email" instead.
5. Observe that it now works, indicating a case mismatch issue.


In our case, this caused our saml IDP connection to fail due to the mapper not mapping the email correctly.

### Anything else?

Proposed Fix:
Remove the forced lowercasing when storing method names:
```java
ACCESSORS.put(propertyName, method);
```
This preserves the original method names and avoids unexpected failures for properties with uppercase letters
commit_message:
Make ProtocolMappers case insensitive

Closes #37577

Signed-off-by: Sebastian Łaskawiec <sebastian.laskawiec@defenseunicorns.com>
Signed-off-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>
Co-authored-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>

issue_title: `ClientPolicyProvider` doesn't check for deleted Clients - throws NPE
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

`ClientPolicyProvider` doesn't check to see if a Client entity has been deleted, causing an NPE in policy evaluation. This can effect many functions, as policy evaluations are triggered by many features.
- This is because the `ClientPolicyRepresentation` is not updated when the Clients they relate to are deleted.
- This is also because the `ClientPolicyProvider` doesn't do null checks on the loaded entities.



### Version

26.1.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

- `ClientPolicyProvider` **MUST** do null checks on the related `ClientModel`s and not throw Exceptions.
- `ClientPolicyRepresentation`s **SHOULD** be updated when their related Clients are deleted.

### Actual behavior

`ClientPolicyProvider` throws an NPE when related Client entities are missing.

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/authz/policy/common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/policy/provider/client/ClientPolicyProvider.java#L32-L34


### How to Reproduce?

1. Add Client a Client Policy using the policy UI
2. Delete the Client using the Client UI
3. Many APIs (e.g. User search) fails because `ClientPolicyProvider` throws an NPE


### Anything else?

_No response_

Add a null check to ClientModels loaded by ID from ClientPolicyRepresentation.

Closes #37673

issue_title: Keycloak Fails to Load HTTPS Key Material (Incorrect Path Resolution)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

**Environment:**  
- **OS:** Debian 5.10.226-1 (aarch64)  
- **Keycloak Version:** 26.1.2 (Quarkus distribution)  
- **Installation Path:** `/home/sumonst21/apps/keycloak-qarkus/keycloak-26.1.2`  
- **Command Executed:** `bin/kc.sh start`  

**Issue:**  
Keycloak fails to start due to an incorrect path resolution for the HTTPS certificate file. The error references `..conf/server.crt.pem` instead of `../conf/server.crt.pem`, causing a `NoSuchFileException`.  

**Error Output:**  
```bash
Failed to load 'https-key-' material: NoSuchFileException /home/sumonst21/apps/keycloak-qarkus/keycloak-26.1.2/bin/..conf/server.crt.pem
```

**Diagnostic Steps Taken:**  
1. Checked if the file exists using `stat`:  
   ```bash
   stat /home/sumonst21/apps/keycloak-qarkus/keycloak-26.1.2/bin/..conf/server.crt.pem
   ```
   Output:  
   ```bash
   stat: cannot statx '/home/sumonst21/apps/keycloak-qarkus/keycloak-26.1.2/bin/..conf/server.crt.pem': No such file or directory
   ```

2. Checked the correct resolved path:  
   ```bash
   stat /home/sumonst21/apps/keycloak-qarkus/keycloak-26.1.2/bin/../conf/server.crt.pem
   ```
   Output:  
   ```bash
   File: /home/sumonst21/apps/keycloak-qarkus/keycloak-26.1.2/bin/../conf/server.crt.pem -> /etc/letsencrypt/live/auth.x-helsinki1-1/fullchain.pem
   ```

3. Verified file permissions:  
   - The correct symbolic link exists (`../conf/server.crt.pem`).  
   - Keycloak is attempting to load an incorrect path (`..conf/server.crt.pem`).  

### Version

26.1.2 (Quarkus distribution) 

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should correctly resolve `../conf/server.crt.pem` instead of `..conf/server.crt.pem`.  


### Actual behavior

Keycloak is misinterpreting the relative path, leading to a file not found error.  


### How to Reproduce?

**Steps to Reproduce:**  
1. Keep the cert path config in `conf/keycloak.conf` default
1. Create a symbolic link from `conf/server.crt.pem` to a Let's Encrypt certificate (`fullchain.pem`). (optional, since the error caused by incorrect path resoluation wth `..` joined with `conf` path without a `/`)
2. Run `bin/kc.sh start`.  
3. Observe the error output.  



### Anything else?

**Suggested Fix:**  
- Review Keycloak's path resolution logic in `kc.sh`.  
- Ensure `https-key` configuration correctly resolves `../conf/server.crt.pem`.

issue_title: Session type incorrectly set in access-token context when token created with scope=offline_access
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Follow-up to #37118 .

When the offline token is issued from browser flow or from resource-owner-password-credentials flow, the session-type in the access-token is incorrectly set as `on` when it should be rather `of` as it is offline session. The session-type are first 2 characters of access-token ID (claim `jti`).

### Version

nightly (from 2025-02-27)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Session type set to `of` whenever it is linked with offline session

### Actual behavior

Session type set to `on` even if it is linked with offline session

See steps to reproduce

```
2025-02-28 01:04:36,008 DEBUG [org.keycloak.storage.ldap.mappers.msad.MSADUserAccountControlStorageMapper] (executor-thread-9) Going to propagate enabled=true for ldapUser 'cn=testuser,OU=Users,OU=TEST,DC=test,DC=company,DC=com' to MSAD

```

### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure LDAP connection to Active Directory (I tested using a multi domain controller AWS Managed Microsoft AD) and ensure a msad-user-account-control-mapper is configured.
2. Attempt to create a user in Keycloak

### Anything else?

We upgraded from 26.0.1 to 26.1.2, at which point we started experiencing errors when creating users. In my investigation, I believe this is due to the changes made in PR [34470](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/34470). Prior to this change, the `userAccountControl` wouldn't be modified at create time. I suspect the `ldapUser` in `setEnabled` is not updated with the real `userAccountControl` value from AD. 

If a sync is introduced as part of the creation flow before `setEnabled` is run, it should be noted that AD implements eventual consistency and care should be taken to avoid introducing an issue similar to the UUID issue addressed in [29206](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/29206) and fixed in PR [29470](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/29470).
